# MongoDB Connector - Full Documentation

# MongoDB Connector 6.3

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Anypoint Connector for MongoDB (MongoDB Connector) is a closed-source connector that provides a
connection between the Mule runtime engine (Mule) and third-party software on a MongoDB server.

Release Notes:
[MongoDB Connector Release Notes](../../release-notes/connector/mongodb-connector-release-notes-mule-4)  
Exchange:
[MongoDB Connector](https://www.mulesoft.com/exchange/com.mulesoft.connectors/mule-mongodb-connector/)

## Before You Begin

To use this connector, you must be familiar with MongoDB, Mule runtime engine (Mule), Anypoint
Connectors, Anypoint Studio, elements in a Mule flow, and global elements.

Before creating an app, you must have access to the MongoDB target resource and Anypoint Platform.
You must also understand how to create a Mule app using Anypoint Studio.

To use the this connector, you need:

- A MongoDB instance
- A set of valid credentials, including the required MongoDB endpoints that point to your instance

## Common Use Cases for the Connector

Common use cases for MongoDB Connector include:

- **Single View of Data**

  Create a single view of your most important data and aggregate your siloed data from multiple
  enterprise systems, transform the data, and write the data to MongoDB. For example, you can import
  data into a CSV format from a local directory into MongoDB.

- **Mainframe Offloading**

  Mirror or synchronize your operational data from mainframes into MongoDB to offload workloads from
  your mainframes while enabling the ability to meet your customer where they are.

- **Real-time Analytics**

  Leverage MongoDBâs rich query language to make real-time decisions on shipping, inventory, and
  pricing, and analyze any data using documents in MongoDB.

- **Personalization**

  Create personalized omnichannel retail experiences by collecting and synchronizing data across
  your retail channels and your backend systems into MongoDB.

For examples of these use cases, refer to [MongoDB Connector Examples](mongodb-connector-examples).

## Authentication

This connector does not validate all possible authentication types.

## Exchange Example

[Anypoint Exchange](https://www.mulesoft.com/exchange/org.mule.examples/importing-a-CSV-file-into-Mongo-DB/)
has an example that shows how to import data in a CSV format from a local directory into MongoDB.

## Important Date Handling Considerations

When using MongoDB Connector, consider the following date-handling behavior:

- **Automatic conversion to UTC**

  MongoDB Connector converts dates (whether sent to or retrieved from MongoDB) to UTC before storage
  for consistent date handling in distributed environments.

- **ISO 8601 format**

  Dates (whether they are sent to or retrieved from MongoDB) in JSON documents are transformed to
  the ISO 8601 format. Additionally, any String values in JSON documents that match the ISO 8601
  format are automatically transformed and stored as Date types in MongoDB. This change ensures more
  consistent and standardized date handling in a distributed environment.

- **Millisecond truncation**

  Dates (whether sent to or retrieved from MongoDB) that include milliseconds with a value of `.000`
  are not shown in connector output.

Here is an example of date handling in a Mule app:

Input:

```json
{
  "timeStamp": "2024-06-17T16:44:17.000+01:00"
}
```

Mule 4:

```json
{
  "timeStamp": "2024-06-17T15:44:17+00:00"
}
```

Because of these changes in date handling, it is important to:

- **Review flows involving dates**

  If your Mule apps rely on specific timezones of dates stored in MongoDB, you must review and
  possibly adjust flows to correctly handle the UTC conversion.

- **Test thoroughly**

  Conduct thorough testing in a controlled environment to ensure that the changes in date handling
  do not negatively impact the expected behavior of the Mule app.

- **Document the behavioral differences**

  Review these date-handling details to ensure correct behavior in your application.

## Next Steps

After you complete the prerequisites and experiment with the example, you are ready to create an app
with [Studio](mongodb-connector-studio).

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/intro-use-exchange)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# MongoDB Connector Additional Configuration Information

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

## Additional Results in the Response of the Execute Command Operation

The **Execute Command** operation returns one page of results. If an execution returns a cursor
reference, invoke the `getMore` command as needed to traverse subsequent pages of results.

Suppose `executeCommand` is invoked with the following command value:

```dataweave
{
	"find": ("test"),
	"skip": 0,
	"batchSize": 10
}
```

The `executeCommand` returns a result like this:

```dataweave
{
  "cursor": {
    "firstBatch": [
      {
        "_id": {
          "$oid": "..."
        },
        "meta": {
          "view": {
            "id": 1,
//            ...
           }
//         ,{ ... }, ...
        }
//....
      }
    ],
    "id": 9041685167132828464,
    "ns": "test.test"
  },
  "ok": 1.0
}
```

To get additional results until there are none left, use the
[`getMore` command](https://www.mongodb.com/docs/v4.4/reference/command/getmore/) to retrieve the
next page of results:

```dataweave
{
	"getMore": 9041685167132828464,
	"collection" : "test",
	"batchSize": 10
}
```

The results of the `getMore` command include a cursor ID to use in subsequent `getMore` commands.

## See Also

[Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# MongoDB Connector 6.3 Examples

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

## Invoke an Operation

To invoke a simple operation, such as the **Insert Document** operation, follow these steps:

1.  In Studio, click **HTTP** and drag the **Listener** operation to the canvas.
2.  From the palette, select **MongoDB** and drag the Insert Document operation into your flow by
    placing it to the right of **HTTP Listener**.
3.  Configure the connector by selecting the **Connector Configuration** you created in the previous
    section, choosing the operation to invoke, and setting the **Collection name**.
4.  From the palette, drag the **Transform Message** component to the left of MongoDB Connector.
5.  Click **Transform Message** and enter two key-value pairs:

    ```dataweave
    %dw 2.0
    %output application/json
    ---
    {
    	name:"Peter",
    	age:"42"
    }
    ```

6.  From the Mule Palette view, drag other **Transform Message** components to the right of MongoDB
    Connector.
7.  Click **Transform Message** and set the output to `application/json`:

    ```dataweave
    %dw 2.0
    output application/json
    ---
    payload
    ```

### XML for Invoke an Operation

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<mule xmlns:mongo="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/mongo"
	xmlns:ee="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core"
	xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http"
	xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core"
	xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation"
	xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
	xsi:schemaLocation="
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core/current/mule-ee.xsd
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core/current/mule-ee.xsd
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/mongo
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/mongo/current/mule-mongo.xsd">

	<configuration-properties file="mule-app.properties" />
	<http:listener-config name="HTTP_Listener_config"
		doc:name="HTTP Listener config">
		<http:listener-connection host="0.0.0.0"
			port="8081" />
	</http:listener-config>
	<mongo:config name="MongoDB_Config" doc:name="MongoDB Config">
		<mongo:connection database="${database}">
			<mongo:server-addresses >
				<mongo:server-address host="${host}" port="${port}"/>
			</mongo:server-addresses>
		</mongo:connection>
    </mongo:config>

    <flow name="create-mongo-record-flow" >
        <http:listener
				  config-ref="HTTP_Listener_config" path="/create"
              doc:name="Listener" />
        <ee:transform doc:name="Set Payload and Document Content">
            <ee:message>
                <ee:set-payload><![CDATA[%dw 2.0
output application/json
---
{
	name:"Pity",
	age:"31"
}]]></ee:set-payload>
            </ee:message>
        </ee:transform>
        <mongo:insert-document config-ref="MongoDB_Config"
               doc:name="Insert document"
               collectionName="${collection}" />
        <logger level="INFO" doc:name="Logger"
	  message="New document:  #[payload]" />
    </flow>
</mule>
```

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)

# MongoDB Connector 6.3 Reference

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Anypoint Connector for MongoDB (MongoDB Connector) is a closed-source connector that provides a
connection between the Mule runtime engine (Mule) and third-party software on a MongoDB server.

## Configuration

Default Configuration for MongoDB Connector.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name for this configuration. Connectors reference the configuration with this name.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Connection</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#config_connection">Connection</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#config_connection-string">Connection String</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Connection types for this configuration.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Read Concern</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>LOCAL</p></li><li><p>AVAILABLE</p></li><li><p>MAJORITY</p></li><li><p>LINEARIZABLE</p></li><li><p>SNAPSHOT</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Enables you to control the consistency and isolation properties of the data read from replica sets and replica set shards.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Read Preference</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>PRIMARY</p></li><li><p>PRIMARY_PREFERED</p></li><li><p>SECONDARY</p></li><li><p>SECONDARY_PREFERRED</p></li><li><p>NEAREST</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the read preferences for this connection.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>PRIMARY</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Maximum Staleness Seconds</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies, in seconds, how stale a secondary node on a MongoDB cluster is before the client stops using the secondary node for read operations.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>-1</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Read Preference Tags</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ReadPreferenceTag">Read Preference Tag</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If replica set members are associated with tags, you can specify a tag set (array of tag specification documents) in the read preference to target those members.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Encoding</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the encoding to use in the connector. If unspecified, the default for Mule is used.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Expiration Policy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ExpirationPolicy">Expiration Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures the minimum amount of time that a dynamic configuration instance can remain idle before Mule considers it eligible for expiration.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Write Concern Acknowledgement</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Level of acknowledgment requested from MongoDB for write operations propagated to the specified number of MongoDB instances. You can specify a number of instances, for example, <code>n&gt;0</code>, or use a value from the list.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Write Concern Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies a time limit, in milliseconds, for <strong>Write Concern Acknowledgement</strong>. The value must be greater than <code>1</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Write Concern Timeout Timeunit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Write Concern Timeout</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>SECONDS</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Connection Type

Uses a database, a username, and a password for connection.

> [!NOTE] This connector does not validate all possible authentication types.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Servers (host:port)</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ServerAddress">Server Address</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Server in which your MongoDB instance is located. You can specify multiple servers.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Database</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Database name to which you want to connect.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Username</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Username to use for authentication.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Password to use for authentication. If you do not want to use authentication, the password and the username must both be empty.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Replica Set Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the replica set to which to connect. Use this field to connect to a MongoDB cluster.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Authentication Source</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Authentication source.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Compressors</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>List of compressors to enable network compression for communication between the client and a mongod or mongos instance. If you specify multiple compressors, then the order in which you list the compressors matters. The driver uses the first compressor in the list.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Zlib Compression Level</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Integer that specifies the compression level if using zlib for network compression. <code>0</code> means no compression. <code>1</code> through <code>9</code> are the compression levels, with <code>1</code> being the lowest compression level, and <code>9</code> being the highest compression level. Higher compression levels take more time to process than lower compression levels.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>-1</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Connection Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How long the connector waits before timing out when establishing a connection to the remote service.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>30</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Connection Timeout Timeunit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Connection Timeout</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>SECONDS</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Local Threshold</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Size of the latency window for selecting among multiple suitable MongoDB instances.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>15</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Local Threshold Time Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Local Threshold</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>MILLISECONDS</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Server Selection Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How long to block for server selection before throwing an exception.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>30</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Server Selection Timeout Time Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Server Selection Timeout</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>SECONDS</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Socket Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Socket timeout. Used for I/O socket read and write operations. Set this field to <code>0</code> if you do not want socket read and write operations to time out.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Socket Timeout Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Socket Timeout</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>SECONDS</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Retry Writes</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Enable retryable writes.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>false</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Authentication Mechanism</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>PLAIN</p></li><li><p>SCRAM_SHA_1</p></li><li><p>SCRAM_SHA_256</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Authentication mechanism used for this connection.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Tls Context</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">Tls</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures TLS. If using the HTTPS protocol, you must configure TLS.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Min Connection Pool Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Minimum size for the connection pool.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Max Connection Pool Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Maximum size for the connection pool.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>100</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Max Wait Queue Time</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Maximum wait queue time for the connection pool.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>120</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Max Wait Queue Time Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Max Wait Queue Time</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>SECONDS</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Max Connection Life Time</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Maximum connection lifetime for the connection pool.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Max Connection Life Time Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Max Connection Life Time</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>SECONDS</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Max Connection Idle Time</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Maximum connection idle time for the connection pool.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Max Connection Idle Time Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Max Connection Idle Time</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>SECONDS</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures a reconnection strategy to use when a connector operation fails to connect to an external server.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Connection String Connection Type

Uses a connection string for connection.

> [!NOTE] This connector does not validate all possible authentication types.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Connection String</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>URI that describes the hosts to use and their options.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Tls Context</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">Tls</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures TLS. If using the HTTPS protocol, you must configure TLS.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures a reconnection strategy to use when a connector operation fails to connect to an external server.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Sources

- [On Object Listener](#object-listener)

### On Object Listener

`<mongo:object-listener>`

Source for object events.

> [!NOTE] This source (listener) uses Object Store to save watermarks. You might experience
> limitations that are specific to the Object Store implementation you are using (Object Store for
> CloudHub deployments or Object Store for on-premises deployments), so configure Object Store to
> suit your needs.  
> For more information, see
> [The Different Types of Object Stores Explained](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=001123185&type=1).

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Collection Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Collection that is watched to detect the matching documents.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Watermark Field</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Field that is queried to detect the objects that match the trigger condition.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Primary Node Only</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Determines whether to execute this source on only the primary node when running Mule instances in a cluster.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Scheduling Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>scheduling-strategy</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures the scheduler that triggers the polling.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Streaming Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#repeatable-in-memory-stream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#repeatable-file-store-stream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Redelivery Policy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RedeliveryPolicy">Redelivery Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Defines a policy for processing the redelivery of the same message.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Binary</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Associated Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)
