- [Standard Revocation Check](#standard-revocation-check)
- [Tls](#Tls)
- [Trust Store](#TrustStore)

### Bulk Item

Bulk item type.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Exception</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Exception.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>ID.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Message</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Message.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Payload</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Payload.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Status Code</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Status code.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Successful</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Indicates success.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Bulk Operation Result

Bulk operation result type.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>ID.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Items</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#BulkItem">Bulk Item</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Array of items.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Successful</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Indicates success.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Crl File

Specifies the location of the certification revocation list (CRL) file.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Path to the CRL file.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Custom Ocsp Responder

Configures a custom OCSP responder for certification revocation checks.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Url</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>URL of the OCSP responder.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Cert Alias</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Alias of the signing certificate for the OCSP response. If specified, the alias must be in the truststore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Expiration Policy

Configures an expiration policy strategy.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Max Idle Time</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures the maximum amount of time that a dynamic configuration instance can remain idle before Mule considers it eligible for expiration.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Time Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Max Idle Time</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Index

Index type.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Field Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Field name.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the index.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Order</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>ASC</p></li><li><p>DESC</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Order of the index.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Key Store

Configures the keystore for the TLS protocol. The keystore you generate contains a private key and a
public certificate.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Path to the keystore. Mule resolves the path relative to the current classpath and file system.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Type of keystore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Alias</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Alias of the key to use when the keystore contains multiple private keys. By default, Mule uses the first key in the file.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Key Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Password used to protect the private key.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Password used to protect the keystore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Algorithm</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Encryption algorithm that the keystore uses.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Read Preference Tag

Read preference tag type.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Name Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name value.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnect

Configures a standard reconnection strategy, which specifies how often to reconnect and how many
reconnection attempts the connector source or operation can make.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Frequency</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How often to attempt to reconnect, in milliseconds.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Blocking</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If <code>false</code>, the reconnection strategy runs in a separate, non-blocking thread.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Count</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How many reconnection attempts the Mule app can make.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnect Forever

Configures a forever reconnection strategy by which the connector source or operation attempts to
reconnect at a specified frequency for as long as the Mule app runs.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Frequency</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How often to attempt to reconnect, in milliseconds.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnection

Configures a reconnection strategy for an operation.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Fails Deployment</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>What to do if, when an app is deployed, a connectivity test does not pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>true</code></p><div><p>Allow the deployment to fail.</p></div></li><li><p><code>false</code></p><div><p>Ignore the results of the connectivity test.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Reconnection strategy to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Redelivery Policy

Configures the redelivery policy for executing requests that generate errors. You can add a
redelivery policy to any source in a flow.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Max Redelivery Count</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Maximum number of times that a redelivered request can be processed unsuccessfully before returning a <code>REDELIVERY_EXHAUSTED</code> error.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Message Digest Algorithm</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Secure hashing algorithm to use if the <strong>Use Secure Hash</strong> field is <code>true</code>. If the payload of the message is a Java object, Mule ignores this value and returns the value that the payloadâs <code>hashCode()</code> returned.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Id Expression</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>One or more expressions that determine if a message is redelivered. This property can be set only if the <strong>Use Secure Hash</strong> field is <code>false</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Object Store</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>ObjectStore</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures the object store that stores the redelivery counter for each message.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Repeatable File Store Iterable

Repeatable file store iterable type.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>In Memory Objects</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Maximum amount of instances to keep in memory. If more than that is required, content on the disk is buffered.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Buffer Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>BYTE</p></li><li><p>KB</p></li><li><p>MB</p></li><li><p>GB</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Unit for the <strong>In Memory Size</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Repeatable File Store Stream

Configures the repeatable file-store streaming strategy by which Mule keeps a portion of the stream
content in memory. If the stream content is larger than the configured buffer size, Mule backs up
the bufferâs content to disk and then clears the memory.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>In Memory Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Maximum amount of memory that the stream can use for data. If the amount of memory exceeds this value, Mule buffers the content to disk. To optimize performance:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>Configure a larger buffer size to avoid the number of times Mule needs to write the buffer on disk. This increases performance, but it also limits the number of concurrent requests your application can process, because it requires additional memory.</p></li><li><p>Configure a smaller buffer size to decrease memory load at the expense of response time.</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Buffer Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>BYTE</p></li><li><p>KB</p></li><li><p>MB</p></li><li><p>GB</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Unit for the <strong>In Memory Size</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Repeatable In Memory Iterable

Repeatable in memory iterable type.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Initial Buffer Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Amount of instances to initially keep in memory to consume the stream and provide random access to it. If the stream contains more data than can fit into this buffer, then the buffer expands according to the <strong>Buffer Size Increment</strong> attribute, with an upper limit of <strong>Max In Memory Size</strong>. Default value is 100 instances.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Buffer Size Increment</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>This is by how much the buffer size expands if it exceeds its initial size. Setting a value of <code>0</code> or lower means that the buffer must not expand, meaning that a <code>STREAM_MAXIMUM_SIZE_EXCEEDED</code> error is raised when the buffer gets full. Default value is 100 instances.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Max Buffer Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Maximum amount of memory to use. If more than that is used, a <code>STREAM_MAXIMUM_SIZE_EXCEEDED</code> error is raised. A value lower than or equal to <code>0</code> means no limit.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Repeatable In Memory Stream

Configures the in-memory streaming strategy by which the request fails if the data exceeds the MAX
buffer size. Always run performance tests to find the optimal buffer size for your specific use
case.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Initial Buffer Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Initial amount of memory to allocate to the data stream. If the streamed data exceeds this value, the buffer expands by <strong>Buffer Size Increment</strong>, with an upper limit of <strong>Max In Memory Size value</strong>.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Buffer Size Increment</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Amount by which the buffer size expands if it exceeds its initial size. Setting a value of <code>0</code> or lower specifies that the buffer canât expand.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Max Buffer Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The maximum amount of memory to use. If more than that is used then a <code>STREAM_MAXIMUM_SIZE_EXCEEDED</code> error is raised. A value lower than or equal to zero means no limit.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Buffer Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>BYTE</p></li><li><p>KB</p></li><li><p>MB</p></li><li><p>GB</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Unit for the <strong>Initial Buffer Size</strong>, <strong>Buffer Size Increment</strong>, and <strong>Buffer Unit</strong> fields.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Server Address

Server address type.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Host</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Host to which you want to connect.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Port</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Port of the host to which you want to connect.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>27017</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Standard Revocation Check

Configures standard revocation checks for TLS certificates.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Only End Entities</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Which elements to verify in the certificate chain:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>true</code></p><div><p>Verify only the last element in the certificate chain.</p></div></li><li><p><code>false</code></p><div><p>Verify all elements in the certificate chain.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Prefer Crls</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How to check certificate validity:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>true</code></p><div><p>Check the Certification Revocation List (CRL) for certificate validity.</p></div></li><li><p><code>false</code></p><div><p>Use the Online Certificate Status Protocol (OCSP) to check certificate validity.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>No Fallback</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Whether to use the secondary method to check certificate validity:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>true</code></p><div><p>Use the method that wasnât specified in the <strong>Prefer Crls</strong> field (the secondary method) to check certificate validity.</p></div></li><li><p><code>false</code></p><div><p>Do not use the secondary method to check certificate validity.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Soft Fail</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>What to do if the revocation server canât be reached or is busy:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>true</code></p><div><p>Avoid verification failure.</p></div></li><li><p><code>false</code></p><div><p>Allow the verification to fail.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Tls

Configures TLS to provide secure communications for the Mule app.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Enabled Protocols</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Comma-separated list of protocols enabled for this context.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Enabled Cipher Suites</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Comma-separated list of cipher suites enabled for this context.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Trust Store</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#TrustStore">Trust Store</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures the TLS truststore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Key Store</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#KeyStore">Key Store</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures the TLS keystore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Revocation Check</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#standard-revocation-check">Standard Revocation Check</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#custom-ocsp-responder">Custom Ocsp Responder</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#crl-file">Crl File</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures a revocation checking mechanism.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Trust Store

Configures the truststore for TLS.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Path to the truststore. Mule resolves the path relative to the current classpath and file system.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Password used to protect the truststore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Type of truststore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Algorithm</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Encryption algorithm that the truststore uses.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Insecure</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If <code>true</code>, Mule stops performing certificate validations. Setting this to <code>true</code> can make connections vulnerable to attacks.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Using Anypoint Studio to Configure MongoDB Connector 6.3

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Anypoint Studio (Studio) editors help you design and update your Mule applications, properties, and
configuration files.

To add and configure a connector in Studio:

1.  [Create a Mule project](#create-mule-project).
2.  [Add the connector to your Mule project](#add-connector-to-project).
3.  [Configure an input source for the connectorâs flow](#configure-input-source).
4.  [Add a connector operation to the flow](#add-connector-operation).
5.  [Configure a global element for the connector](#configure-global-element).
6.  [Configure the other connector fields](#configure-other-fields).

If you are new to configuring connectors in Studio, see
[Using Anypoint Studio to Configure a Connector](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-use-studio).
If, after reading this topic, you need additional information about the connector fields, see the
[MongoDB Connector Reference](mongodb-connector-reference).

## Create a Mule Project

In Studio, create a new Mule project in which to add and configure the connector:

1.  In Studio, select **File > New > Mule Project**.
2.  Enter a name for your Mule project and click **Finish**.

## Add the Connector to Your Mule Project

Add <connector name> to your Mule project to automatically populate the XML code with the
connectorâs namespace and schema location and add the required dependencies to the projectâs
`pom.xml` file:

1.  In the **Mule Palette** view, click **(X) Search in Exchange**.
2.  In **Add Modules to Project**, type `mongodb` in the search field.
3.  Click **MongoDB** in **Available modules**.
4.  Click **Add**.
5.  Click **Finish**.

Adding a connector to a Mule project in Studio does not make that connector available to other
projects in your Studio workspace.

## Configure an Input Source for the Connector

Configure an input source for the connector, such as the Object Listener operation, HTTP Listener,
or Scheduler. The Object Listener operation polls the given MongoDB collection for new documents
that apply to the filter and invokes your app when it finds one.

For example, to configure `HTTP Listener`, follow these steps:

1.  In the **Mule Palette** view, select **HTTP** > **Listener**.
2.  Drag **Listener** to the Studio canvas.
3.  On the **Listener** configuration screen, optionally change the value of the **Display Name**
    field.
4.  Specify a value for the **Path** field.
5.  Click the plus sign (+) next to the **Connector configuration** field to configure a global
    element that can be used by all instances of `HTTP Listener` in the app.
6.  On the **General** tab, specify connection information.
7.  On the **TLS** tab, optionally specify TLS information.
8.  On the **Advanced** tab, optionally specify reconnection information, including a reconnection
    strategy.
9.  Click **Test Connection** to confirm that Mule can connect with the specific server.
10. Click **OK**.

## Add a Connector Operation to the Flow

When you add a connector operation to your flow, you immediately define a specific operation for
that connector to perform.

To add an operation for MongoDB, follow these steps:

1.  In the **Mule Palette** view, select **MongoDB** and then select the desired operation.
2.  Drag the operation onto the Studio canvas to the right of the input source.

## Configure a Global Element for the Connector

When you configure a connector, itâs best to configure a global element that all instances of that
connector in the app can use. Configuring a global element requires you to provide the
authentication credentials the connector uses to access the target MongoDB system.

To configure the global element for MongoDB Connector, follow these steps:

1.  On the **Global Elements** tab, click **Create**.
2.  Expand **Connector Configuration** and search for `MongoDB config`.
3.  Click **OK**.
4.  Enter a value for the following properties:
    - **Servers (host:port)**

      Servers where your MongoDB instance is located, as specified by one or more host:port pairs.
      The default value is `localhost:27017`. Follow these steps to add one or more host:port
      instances:
      1.  Select **Edit inline**.
      2.  Click the green plus sign (+).
      3.  Add the host:port instance and click **Finish**.
      4.  Repeat steps 2 and 3 for each host:port instance you want to add.

    - **Database**

      Database that sends and retrieves data from the MongoDB instance

    - **Username**

      Username to access the MongoDB instance

    - **Password**

      Corresponding password

Use the MongoDB configuration with Spring property placeholders for the **Connection** field values
when you plan to deploy your application to CloudHub or to Mule runtime engine. You can hard code
your connection credentials in the global element properties if you are in the development stage and
want to accelerate the development process.

## Global Configuration with SSL (Optional)

MongoDB Connector v6.0.0 and later supports the use of SSL, if your MongoDB instance supports it. To
set up the global configuration for SSL, select the **TLS context** field in the **Security** tab in
your MongoDB connection.

## Logging

By default, logging is enabled via SLF4J API. The driver uses the following logger names:

- `org.mongodb.driver`, the root logger
  - `cluster`, for logs related to monitoring of the MongoDB servers to which the driver connects
  - `connection`, for logs related to connections and connection pools
  - `protocol`, for logs related to protocol message sent to and received from a MongoDB server
    - `insert`, for logs related to insert messages and responses
    - `update`, for logs related to update messages and responses
    - `delete`, for logs related to delete messages and responses
    - `query`, for logs related to query messages and responses
    - `getmore`, for logs related to getmore messages and responses
    - `killcursor`, for logs related to killcursor messages and responses
    - `command`, for logs related to command messages and responses

  - `uri`, for logs related to connection string parsing
  - `management`, for logs related to JMX

## Next Steps

After configuring Studio, see the [Examples](mongodb-connector-examples) topic for more
configuration ideas.

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Troubleshooting MongoDB Connector 6.3

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

To troubleshoot Anypoint Connector for MongoDB (MongoDB Connector), become familiar with the
information about interpreting commonly thrown exception messages.

## Enable SSL Logging

Enable SSL logs to investigate an issue related to TLS communications.

Set your SSL log level depending on your required level of detail:

1.  Enable only SSL log details:

    `javax.net.debug=ssl`

2.  Enable only handshaking details. (The handshake protocol is a series of messages exchanged over
    the record protocol.)

    `javax.net.debug=ssl:handshake`

3.  Enable handshaking details and SSL details at the same time:

    `javax.net.debug=ssl, handshake`

4.  Enable the dumping of all details and traffic data:

    `javax.net.debug=all`

    > [!NOTE] This option is very verbose and under normal circumstances is not necessary.

> [!WARNING] SSL logging results in a performance impact for HTTPS or other TLS connections. Enable
> SSL logging only to troubleshoot a specific issue and do not enable it for extended periods. SSL
> logging produces a significant amount of log messages and can inundate your log file if it is left
> enabled and unattended.

To enable SSL logs:

Set the debug parameter as an argument in the runtime configuration for the application.

1.  In Studio, right-click on the project and select **Run > Run Configurations**.
2.  Go to the **Arguments** tab and add `-M-Djavax.net.debug=ssl` in the **VM arguments** section.

## Enable Verbose Exception Logging

Enable verbose exception logs to show a complete stack trace of the error instead of the default
truncated output:

1.  In Studio, right-click on the project and select **Run > Run Configurations**.
2.  Go to the **Arguments** tab and append the arguments in the **VM arguments** section to add the
    `mule.verbose.exceptions` property. For example:

    `-XX:PermSize=128M -XX:MaxPermSize=256M -Dmule.verbose.exceptions=true`

## Enable Verbose Logging

To get a better understanding of why an applicationâs interaction with MongoDB Connector is failing,
temporarily enable verbose logging for the connector.

Remember to always disable enhanced verbosity after troubleshooting, because it can affect your Mule
applicationâs performance.

To enable verbose logging in the configuration file:

1.  Access Anypoint Studio and navigate to the **Package Explorer** view.
2.  Open your applicationâs project name.
3.  Open the `src/main/resources` folder.
4.  Open the `log4j2.xml` file inside the folder.
5.  Add an `<AsyncLogger>` tag inside the `<Loggers>` tag:

    ```xml
    			<Loggers>
    				...
    				<AsyncLogger name="com.mulesoft.connectors.mongodb" level="DEBUG"/>
    				...
    			</Loggers>
    ```

6.  Save your application changes.
7.  Click the project name in **Package Explorer** and then click **Run** > **Run As** > **Mule
    Application**.

## Understand Commonly Thrown Exceptions

Here is a list of commonly thrown exception messages and how to interpret them:

- MONGO:CONNECTIVITY

  Either a connection could not be established or there is a connectivity error.

- MONGO:CONSISTENCY_ERROR

  The data is not consistent because the client cannot find a read after performing a write.

- MONGO:DUPLICATE_ERROR

  There is a duplicate key.

- MONGO:INVALID_INPUT

  The provided input data for an operation is not valid.

- MONGO:NOT_FOUND

  The server could not find the requested resource.

- MONGO:PERMISSIONS_DENIED

  A permission error occurred while accessing a collection in a database.

- MONGO:RETRY_EXHAUSTED

  The maximum number of retries for the operation is reached.

- MONGO:TIMEOUT

  A specific request takes longer than the configuration timeout values.

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [MongoDB Connector Reference](mongodb-connector-reference)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
- [Known Issues](https://help.salesforce.com/s/issues)
---

# Upgrading and Migrating MongoDB Connector to version 6.x

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Upgrade Anypoint Connector for MongoDB (MongoDB Connector) from version 5.3.x to version 6.x.x.

## Supported Upgrade Paths

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>From Version</th><th>To Version</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>5.3.x</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>6.x.x</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Changes in This Release

MongoDB 6.x.x contains the following changes:

- Changed connection types and configuration
- Changed operation names, metadata, fields, and values
- Changed input sources
- Added the ability to specify which MongoDB Java driver to use
- At runtime, you can overwrite the **Write concern acknowledgement** and **Write concern timeout**
  fields in the global element of the following operations:
  - Find documents
  - Insert document
  - Insert documents
  - Update Documents
  - Count documents operations

### Changes in Connection Types and Configuration

MongoDB 6.x contains the following changes to connection types and configuration:

- Removed the Connection String connection type (reintroduced in 6.3.0 version to support MongoDB
  Atlas)
- Removed the Metadata configuration section

  MongoDB automatically generates the metadata.

#### Config Advanced Tab

The following optional changes apply to the **Advanced** tab in the global configuration:

- Added the following read concern options:
  - Read concern: Enables the control of the consistency and isolation properties of the data read
    from replica sets and replica set shards.
  - Read preference: Specifies the read preferences for this connection.
  - Maximum staleness seconds: Specifies how stale a secondary MongoDB cluster can be before the
    client stops using it for read operations.

    A value of -1 means that there is no maximum staleness threshold.

  - Read preference tags: Enables you to specify a tag set in the read preference to target those
    members associated with a tag in the set.

- Added the following write concern options:
  - Write concern acknowledgment: Requests an acknowledgment that the write operation was propagated
    to the specified number of instances.
  - Write concern timeout: Specifies a time limit for the write concern.
  - Write concern timeout timeunit: Specifies a time unit for the Write concern timeout field.

#### Connection Section

The following changes apply to the tabs in the **Connection** section of the global configuration:

- Added the following fields to the General tab:
  - Required Libraries: Reference to the MongoDB Driver.The version must be 3.11 or later.
  - Server Addresses: This field is now is a list of server addresses to use for the connection.

- Added a Security tab for optional TLS context configuration.
- Added the following optional fields to the Advanced tab:
  - Authentication mechanism: Authentication mechanism used for this connection
  - Replica set name: Name of the replica set to which to connect
  - Authentication source: Database name associated with the userâs credentials
  - Compressors: List of compressors for which to enable network compression for communication
    between this client and a mongod/mongos instance
  - Zlib compression level: Integer that specifies the compression level if using zlib for network
    compression.

    Set this field to 0 for no compression or from 1 through 9 for compression, with 1 being the
    lowest compression level and 9 the highest. High compression levels require more processing time
    than low compression levels.

  - Connection timeout: Connection timeout used when establishing the socket connections
  - Connection timeout time unit: Time unit for the Connection timeout field
  - Local threshold: Size of the latency window for selecting among multiple suitable MongoDB
    instances
  - Local threshold time unit: Time unit for the Local threshold time unit field
  - Server selection timeout: How long to block for server selection before throwing an exception
  - Server selection timeout time unit: Time unit for the Server selection timeout field
  - Socket timeout: Maximum time to wait for a send or receive operation on a socket before the
    attempt times out
  - Socket timeout unit: Time unit for the Socket timeout field
  - Min connection pool size: Minimum size for the connection pool
  - Max connection pool size: Maximum size for the connection pool
  - Max wait queue time: Maximum wait queue time for the connection pool
  - Max wait queue time unit: Time unit for the Maximum wait queue time field
  - Max connection life time: Maximum connection lifetime for the connection pool
  - Max connection life time time unit: Time unit for the Maximum connection life time field
  - Max connection idle time: Maximum connection idle time for the connection pool
  - Max connection idle time unit: Time unit for the Maximum connection idle time field

## Changed Operations, Parameters, and Return Types

The following table shows changes to operation names, input parameters, and return types:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>MongoDB 5.x Operation</th><th>Changes in MongoDB 6.0</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Count documents</p></td><td><div><div><p>Input parameters are now:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>Collection name</code> (String)</p></li><li><p><code>Query</code> (JSON)</p></li></ul></div><div><p>Return type is now Long with the count result.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>Create Collection</p></td><td><div><div><p>Input parameters are now:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>Collection name</code> (String)</p></li><li><p><code>Max objects</code> (Integer)</p></li><li><p><code>Collection size</code> (Integer)</p></li><li><p><code>Collection size data unit</code> (TimeUnit)</p></li></ul></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>Exists collection</p></td><td><p>Operation is now called Collection exists.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Create file from payload</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>Operation is now called Create file.</p></li><li><p>Return type is now JSON.</p></li></ul></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>Create index</p></td><td><div><div><p>Input parameters are now:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>Collection name</code> (String)</p></li><li><p><code>Field name</code> (String)</p></li><li><p><code>Sort order</code> (Enum: ASC or DESC)</p></li></ul></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>Dump</p></td><td><div><div><p>Input parameters are now:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>Output directory</code> (String)</p></li><li><p><code>Output name prefix</code> (String)</p></li><li><p><code>Oplog</code> (no change)</p></li><li><p><code>Operation timeout</code> (Integer)</p></li><li><p><code>Operation timeout unit</code> (TimeUnit)</p></li></ul></div><div><p>Return type is now a List&lt;String&gt; type that points to the created files. Each string is a filePath type.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>Execute command</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>Input parameter is <code>Command</code> (JSON).</p></li><li><p>Return type is now a JSON type that contains the result of the command.</p></li></ul></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>Find documents</p></td><td><div><div><p>Input parameters are now:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>Collection name</code> (String)</p></li><li><p><code>Query</code> (JSON)</p></li><li><p><code>Fields</code> (String)</p></li><li><p><code>Sort by</code> (JSON)</p></li><li><p><code>Page size</code> (Integer)</p></li><li><p><code>Limit</code> (Integer)</p></li></ul></div><div><p>This operation supports pagination. Each item returned as a JSON type.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>Find files</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>Input parameters are now:</p><div><ul><li><p><code>Query</code> (JSON)</p></li><li><p><code>Sort</code> (JSON)</p></li></ul></div></li><li><p>Return type is now a JSON list.</p></li></ul></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>Get file content</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>Input parameter is now <code>File id</code> (JSON).</p></li><li><p>Return type is now a stream with the binary file content. This operation also returns the file information as attributes.</p></li></ul></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>Insert document</p></td><td><p>Return type is now an entire JSON object with the <code>_id</code> object of the created document.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Insert documents</p></td><td><p>Return type is now a Bulk Operation Result, which contains a JSON file that lists each created record and its status.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>List collections</p></td><td><p>Return type is now a List&lt;String&gt; type that contains the names of the collections.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>List indices</p></td><td><p>Operation is now called List indexes.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Map reduce objects</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>Operation is now called Map reduce.</p></li><li><p>Return type is now a JSON type that represents the output specified in the reduce function.</p></li></ul></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>Remove documents</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>Input parameters are now:</p><div><ul><li><p><code>Collection name</code> (String)</p></li><li><p><code>Query</code> (JSON)</p></li></ul></div></li><li><p>Return type is now a Long with the count result.</p></li></ul></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>Remove files</p></td><td><p>Input parameter is now <code>File id</code> (JSON).</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Update documents</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>Input parameters are now:</p><div><ul><li><p><code>Collection name</code> (String)</p></li><li><p><code>Query</code> (JSON)</p></li><li><p><code>Content to update</code> (JSON)</p></li><li><p><code>Multiple update</code> (Boolean: moved to the Advanced tab)</p></li><li><p><code>Upsert</code> (Boolean)</p></li></ul></div></li><li><p>Return type is now a JSON type that contains the following structure:</p><div><ul><li><p>Int Matched</p></li><li><p>Int Modified</p></li><li><p>String Upserted Id</p></li></ul></div></li></ul></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Changed Operations Metadata

MongoDB 5.x generated operations metadata when the user provided a set of documents per collection
from which to take the attributes. MongoDB 6.0 generates metadata automatically, based on the latest
document in each collection.

### Metadata in MongoDB v6.0

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Operation Name</th><th>Input Metadata</th><th>Output Metadata</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Insert Document</p></td><td><p>Document</p></td><td><p>Resolved dynamically based on the selected value of the collection parameter. The connector adds the latest document of the given collection and uses the documentâs structure as input/output metadata.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Insert Documents</p></td><td><p>Document</p></td><td><p>Resolved dynamically based on the selected value of the collection parameter. The connector adds the latest document of the given collection and uses the documentâs structure as input/output metadata.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Update Documents</p></td><td><p>Not applicable</p></td><td><div><div><p>JSON that contains the following structure:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>Int Matched</p></li><li><p>Int Modified</p></li><li><p>JSON upsertedId</p></li></ul></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>Remove Documents</p></td><td><p>Query: JSON Object</p></td><td><p>Not applicable</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Count Documents</p></td><td><p>Query: JSON Object</p></td><td><p>Not applicable</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Find Documents</p></td><td><p>Query: JSON Object</p></td><td><p>Resolved dynamically based on the selected value of the collection parameter. The connector uses the structure of the last document in the given collection.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Create File</p></td><td><p>Not applicable</p></td><td><div><div><p>A JSON object with the following attributes:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>JSON Id</p></li><li><p>String fileName</p></li><li><p>Long Length</p></li><li><p>Int ChunkSize</p></li><li><p>Datetime uploadDate</p></li><li><p>JSON Metadata</p></li></ul></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>Find Files</p></td><td><p>Not applicable</p></td><td><div><div><p>List of JSON objects with the following attributes:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>JSON Id</p></li><li><p>String fileName</p></li><li><p>Long Length</p></li><li><p>Int ChunkSize</p></li><li><p>Datetime uploadDate</p></li><li><p>JSON Metadata</p></li></ul></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Removed Operations

The following operations were removed from the MongoDB connector:

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th colspan="2">Removed from MongoDB 5.x</th><th colspan="2">Can be reproduced in MongoDB 6.0.0 through</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><p>Description</p></td><td><p>Name</p></td><td><p>Description</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Incremental dump</p></td><td><p>Executes an incremental dump of the database</p></td><td><p>Dump</p></td><td><p>Use the Dump operation.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Restore</p></td><td><p>Takes the output from the dump and restores it</p></td><td><p>Restore from file or Restore from directory</p></td><td><p>Restore from file or Restore from directory take the output from the dump file or directory and restore it.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Update documents by function</p></td><td><p>Update documents using a Mongo function</p></td><td><p>Not applicable</p></td><td><p>Not applicable</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Update documents by functions</p></td><td><p>Update documents using one or more Mongo functions</p></td><td><p>Not applicable</p></td><td><p>Not applicable</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Find one and update document</p></td><td><p>Finds and updates the first document that matches a given query</p></td><td><p>Not applicable</p></td><td><p>This operationâs functionality can be reproduced by combining other operations.</p><p>For example, you can invoke the Find documents operation with a limit of 1 document. Then you can invoke the Update documents operation using the ID of the document returned previously.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Save document</p></td><td><p>Inserts or updates a document based on its object ID</p></td><td><p>Not applicable</p></td><td><p>Not applicable</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Find one document</p></td><td><p>Finds the first document that matches a given query</p></td><td><p>Find documents</p></td><td><p>Set the Limit=1 to return one document.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Execute generic command</p></td><td><p>Executes a generic command on the database</p></td><td><p>Execute command</p></td><td><p>Executes a command on the database</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>List files</p></td><td><p>Lists all files that match the given query and sorts them by filename</p></td><td><p>Not applicable</p></td><td><p>Not applicable</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Find one file</p></td><td><p>Returns the first file that matches the given query</p></td><td><p>Find files</p></td><td><p>Specify a specific file to query</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Removed Parameters

The **Num To Skip** field in the **Find Documents** operation is removed from MongoDB Connector 6.x.
The `skip()` method in MongoDB, which is used internally by the **Num To Skip** field, can lead to
performance issues, especially as the offset increases. This is because the server scans through the
results from the beginning to the specified offset before returning any documents, which adds
unnecessary load and slows down the operation.

To optimize pagination and improve performance when migrating to MongoDB Connector 6.x, the
following approaches are recommended:

- Avoid using `skip()` for large offsets.

  Instead of using the **Num To Skip** field, it is more efficient to refine query conditions to
  exclude unwanted documents directly.

- Use range queries.

  Use existing indexes to perform range queries. For example, if your documents have a timestamp or
  a unique ID field (indexed), you can use these fields to filter results directly to avoid scanning
  through unwanted documents.

  ```json
  { "timestamp": { "$gt": ISODate("2024-01-01T00:00:00Z") } }
  ```

- Apply `limit()` for pagination.

  Use the `limit()` method in combination with range queries to control the number of documents
  returned, effectively implementing pagination without the performance issues associated with
  `skip()`.

  ```json
  { "timestamp": { "$gt": ISODate("2024-01-01T00:00:00Z") } }
  .limit(10)
  ```

- Combine filters and sorting.

  Consider ordering documents by an indexed field, which allows for more efficient queries when
  paginating through results.

## Changes in Input Sources

MongoDB 6.0.0 has one input source, Object Listener, which retrieves all of the created documents
that belong to a specific collection.

The Delete Sources and Update Sources input sources were removed.

### Metadata in Object Listener

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Input Metadata</th><th>Output Metadata</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Not applicable</p></td><td><p>The connector resolves output metadata dynamically based on the selected value of the Collection Name parameter. The connector reads the last document of the given collection and uses the documentâs structure as output metadata.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Requirements and Limitations

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Software</th><th>Version</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Mule</p></td><td><p>4.1.1 and later</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>MongoDB</p></td><td><p>MongoDB Java driver 3.11 and later</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Upgrade Prerequisites

Before you perform the upgrade, you must:

1.  Create a backup of your files, data, and configuration in case you need to restore to the
    previous version.
2.  Install MongoDB v6.0 to replace the MongoDB operations that were previously included in MongoDB
    Connector v5.x.

## Upgrade Steps

Follow these steps to perform the upgrade from MongoDB Connector v5.3.x to MongoDB Connector v6.0:

1.  In Anypoint Studio, create a Mule project.
2.  In the Mule Palette view, click **Search in Exchange**.
3.  In the **Add Dependencies to Project** window, enter `MongoDB` in the search field.
4.  In the **Available modules** section, select **MongoDB Connector** and click **Add**.
5.  Click **Finish**.
6.  Verify that the salesforce-connector dependency version is 6.0.0 in the `pom.xml` file in the
    Mule project.

Studio upgrades the connector automatically.

## Verify the Upgrade

After you install the latest version of the connector, follow these steps to verify the upgrade:

1.  In Studio, verify that there are no errors in the **Problems** or **Console** views.
2.  Check the project `pom.xml` file and verify that there are no problems.
3.  Test the connection and verify that the operations work.

## Troubleshooting

If there are problems with caching the parameters and metadata, try restarting Studio.

## Revert the Upgrade

If it is necessary to revert to the previous version of MongoDB Connector, change the
mule-mongodb-connector dependency version 6.0.0 in the projectâs `pom.xml` to the previous version.

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# MongoDB Connector 6.3 XML and Maven Support

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

If you manually code a Mule app in XML either from the Anypoint Studio XML editor or from a text
editor, you can get access to Anypoint Connector for MongoDB (MongoDB Connector) in your app by
adding reference statements to your XML Mule flow and in the Apache Maven `pom.xml` file.

You can avoid the manual process described in this topic by using Studio. When you install MongoDB
Connector in Studio, Studio automatically populates the XML code with the MongoDB Connector
namespace and schema location, and adds a dependency to the `pom.xml` file.

## Add a Namespace for the Connector

Paste the following inside the `<mule>` tag of the header of your configuration XML:

```xml
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/mongodb
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/mongodb/current/mule-mongo.xsd
```

This example shows how the namespace statements are placed in the <mule> XML block:

```xml
<mule xmlns:mongodb="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/mongodb"
	xmlns:ee="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core"
	xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http"
	xmlns:file="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/file"
	xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core"
	xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation"
	xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
	xsi:schemaLocation="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/file
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/file/current/mule-file.xsd
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core/current/mule-ee.xsd
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/mongodb
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/mongodb/current/mule-mongo.xsd">
```

## Add a POM File Dependency

The `pom.xml` Maven file generated by Studio contains dependencies for a Mule app. When you code a
Mule app manually, include this XML snippet in your `pom.xml` file to enable access to this
connector:

```xml
<dependency>
  <groupId>com.mulesoft.connectors</groupId>
  <artifactId>mule-mongodb-connector</artifactId>
  <version>6.x.x</version>
  <classifier>mule-plugin</classifier>
</dependency>
```

To obtain the most up-to-date `pom.xml` file information, access the connector in
[Anypoint Exchange](https://www.mulesoft.com/exchange/) and click **Dependency Snippets**. Replace
`6.x.x` in the `<version>` element with your MongoDB version.

## Next Steps

After completing your namespace and `pom.xml` file, you can try the MongoDB
[Examples](mongodb-connector-examples).

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
